text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Foster Families > Independent Living Skills Instruction 22 Matching Service Providers ARC PORTLAND METRO CLUB IMPACT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides information and referral assistance, weekly social activities, and educational programming to support quality of life. 6929 NE Halsey Street Portland, OR 97213 Eligibility: Specialized information and referral: Unrestricted All other services: Age 18 and older who have intellectual or developmental disabilities Hours: Monday 6pm-8pm Email (360) 726-3033 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BRIDGES OREGON EMPLOYMENT AND INDEPENDENT LIVING SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides support to finding jobs and navigating state and local resources and systems, including Vocational Rehabilitation (VR), and the Office of Developmental Disability Services (ODDS). 475 Cottage Street NE Suite 120 Salem, OR 97301 Eligibility: Deaf, DeafBlind, and Hard of Hearing individuals Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 751-2346 Get Directions Visit Website More Details COOS HEALTH AND WELLNESS COOS COUNTY HEALTH AND WELLNESS BEHAVIORAL HEALTH Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides mental health services, screening and assessment services to access ongoing care. 281 LaClair Street Coos Bay, OR 97420 Eligibility: Varies by service. Unrestricted for counseling. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm, closed holidays (541) 266-6700 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING BURNS HIV COMMUNITY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential case management independent living services to individuals living with HIV. Offers expense assistance for HIV medications, transportation costs to medical care and case management appointments, insurance premium payments, transitional housing, rent payment assistance, dental care, disease management education, referrals to other agencies for basic needs, employment, and substance abuse treatment. 51 W Washington Burns, OR 97720 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 370-2810 401 Get Directions Visit Website More Details EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING LA GRANDE HIV COMMUNITY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides confidential case management independent living services to individuals living with HIV. Offers expense assistance for HIV medications, transportation costs to medical care and case management appointments, insurance premium payments, transitional housing, rent payment assistance, dental care, disease management education, referrals to other agencies for basic needs, employment, and substance abuse treatment. 400 E Scenic Drive The Dalles, OR 97058 Eligibility: Individuals living with HIV Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-3pm Email (541) 276-1037 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIFEWORKS NW LIFEWORKS NW BEAVERTON TRANSITION-AGE YOUTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides individual/group skills training, supported employment/education, supported housing, life skills, and psychiatric treatment for transition-age youth with severe mental illnesses impacting their ability to successfully function in the community and achieve normal developmental milestones. The STRIDE program connects transition-age youth with services that support well-being and independence, including peer support, case management, outreach, and engagement. 10700 SW Beaverton-Hillside Highway Suite 110 Beaverton, OR 97005 Eligibility: Transitional Age Youth services: Ages 16-24 with severe mental illnesses STRIDE program: Ages 14-25 with mental illness Hours: Varies by site Email (503) 641-1475 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIFEWORKS NW LIFEWORKS NW KING TRANSITION-AGE YOUTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides individual/group skills training, supported employment/education, supported housing, life skills, and psychiatric treatment for transition-age youth with severe mental illnesses impacting their ability to successfully function in the community and achieve normal developmental milestones. The STRIDE program connects transition-age youth with services that support well-being and independence, including peer support, case management, outreach, and engagement. 3716 NE Martin Luther King Jr Boulevard Portland, OR 97212 Eligibility: Transitional Age Youth services: Ages 16-24 with severe mental illnesses STRIDE program: Ages 14-25 with mental illness Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-6pm, Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 619-9007 Get Directions Visit Website More Details LIFEWORKS NW LIFEWORKS NW ST JOHNS TRANSITION-AGE YOUTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides individual/group skills training, supported employment/education, supported housing, life skills, and psychiatric treatment for transition-age youth with severe mental illnesses impacting their ability to successfully function in the community and achieve normal developmental milestones. The STRIDE program connects transition-age youth with services that support well-being and independence, including peer support, case management, outreach, and engagement. 8425 N Lombard Street Portland, OR 97203 Eligibility: Transitional Age Youth services: Ages 16-24 with severe mental illnesses STRIDE program: Ages 14-25 with mental illness Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-1pm Email (503) 645-9010 Get Directions Visit Website More Details MV ADVANCEMENTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides employment, alternatives to employment, and residential support. 600 SE Baker Street McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Individuals who have developmental and other disabilities coupled with a disability determination Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-4pm Email (503) 472-2248 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OPPORTUNITY FOUNDATION OF CENTRAL OREGON OPPORTUNITY FOUNDATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides life improvement services for adults with developmental disabilities. Vocational and residential programs include comprehensive employment services, recreational activities, and in-home support 835 E Highway 126 Redmond, OR 97756 Eligibility: Individuals with developmental disabilities Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 548-2611 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON COMMISSION FOR THE BLIND EUGENE FIELD OFFICE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides vocational rehabilitation, independent living skills training, career exploration and general counseling for legally blind adults. Also provides independent living services for visually impaired individuals. Includes orientation and mobility training, techniques of daily living, Braille, counseling, support groups, vocational training, job development, and transition services for blind high school students. Also offers a selection of adaptive items for people with visual impairments. 541 Willamette Avenue #408 Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Varies by service; adults certified legally blind or diagnosed with condition leading to blindness, or age 55 years and above and unable to drive or read newspaper with glasses. Age 55 and older for independent living services. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 686-7990 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON COMMISSION FOR THE BLIND MEDFORD FIELD OFFICE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides vocational rehabilitation, independent living skills training, career exploration and general counseling for legally blind adults. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (503) 378-8478 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OREGON SUPPORTED LIVING PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides 24-hour residential settings and supported living services for people who live independently. Staff help create supportive home environments that foster individual growth and independence while maximizing health and safety. Offers assistance with banking, budgeting, medical care, bill paying, community inclusion, etc. Residential homes are located throughout the Eugene-Springfield area. 412 Pearl Street Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Age 18 and older with developmental disabilities that are eligible for public funding or have sufficient resources for private pay Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm (541) 343-4196 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PEARL BUCK CENTER LIFE ENHANCING ACTIVITIES PROGRAM Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides self-efficacy training, community integration opportunities, and personal, social, and vocational development activities. Participants engage in tasks such as recycling, going out for coffee or lunch in the community, attending concerts and community activities, and learning self-help skills. They are offered opportunities to garden, create art works in a variety of media, learn crafts and cooking, reading, music, and other leisure and cultural offerings. 3690 W 1st Avenue Eugene, OR 97402 Eligibility: Adults age 18 and older with moderate to severe developmental disabilities Hours: Monday-Friday 9:30am-2:30pm Email (541) 484-4666 Get Directions Visit Website More Details UCP OREGON ADULT COMMUNITY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides advocacy, information and referral, residential and vocational supports, brokerage support, and supported employment. The Supported Living Program provides daily living assistance, including live-in care. Support can include medication administration, financial paperwork and budgeting assistance, assistance with chores and cooking, and social supports. 96000 NE Cascades Parkway Suite 400 Portland, OR 97220 Eligibility: Age 18 and older who are experiencing cerebral palsy or other intellectual and developmental disabilities Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-4:30pm Email (503) 777-4166 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WALLOWA VALLEY CENTER FOR WELLNESS ASSERTIVE COMMUNITY TREATMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Assertive Community Treatment (ACT) is an integrated approach for community mental health service delivery. ACT serves outpatients whose symptoms of mental illness result in functioning difficulties in life, work, social relationships, and physical health and wellness. Services include case management, medication management, skills training, and group and social skills support. 606 Medical Parkway Enterprise, OR 97828 Eligibility: Call for information Hours: Monday 8am-5pm, Tuesday/Wednesday 7am-7pm, Thursday 8am-7pm, Friday 8am-noon, Saturday 9am-1pm (541) 426-4524 Get Directions Visit Website More Details ARC PORTLAND METRO CLUB IMPACT BRIDGES OREGON EMPLOYMENT AND INDEPENDENT LIVING SERVICES COOS HEALTH AND WELLNESS COOS COUNTY HEALTH AND WELLNESS BEHAVIORAL HEALTH EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING HIV COMMUNITY SERVICES EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING HIV COMMUNITY SERVICES EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING HIV COMMUNITY SERVICES EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING HIV COMMUNITY SERVICES EASTERN OREGON CENTER FOR INDEPENDENT LIVING HIV COMMUNITY SERVICES LIFEWORKS NW TRANSITION-AGE YOUTH SERVICES LIFEWORKS NW TRANSITION-AGE YOUTH SERVICES LIFEWORKS NW TRANSITION-AGE YOUTH SERVICES MV ADVANCEMENTS MV ADVANCEMENTS OPPORTUNITY FOUNDATION OF CENTRAL OREGON OPPORTUNITY FOUNDATION OREGON COMMISSION FOR THE BLIND EUGENE FIELD OFFICE OREGON COMMISSION FOR THE BLIND MEDFORD FIELD OFFICE OREGON COMMISSION FOR THE BLIND OREGON COMMISSION FOR THE BLIND OREGON COMMISSION FOR THE BLIND REDMOND FIELD OFFICE OREGON COMMISSION FOR THE BLIND SALEM FIELD OFFICE OREGON SUPPORTED LIVING PROGRAM OREGON SUPPORTED LIVING PROGRAM PEARL BUCK CENTER LIFE ENHANCING ACTIVITIES PROGRAM UCP OREGON ADULT COMMUNITY SERVICES WALLOWA VALLEY CENTER FOR WELLNESS ASSERTIVE COMMUNITY TREATMENT
